project.name: StockVision - SahamAI
project.description: StockVision (SahamAI) is a Flask (Python) backend service with a PostgreSQL database that scrapes, processes, and serves broker-activity analysis data for the Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX), integrating trading, broker, insider-transaction, and bid/offer data from Stockbit and RTI. The repository also includes a companion Vue 3 + Vite frontend ("SahamScope") for visualizing this data. Docker packaging (Dockerfiles for backend/frontend, docker-compose.yml, DOCKER.md) was added in a recent merge, and the codebase was reorganized from a flat SahamAI/SahamAI/ layout into backend/ and frontend/ directories.

nodes.name: DOCKER.md
nodes.summary: Indonesian-language Docker integration guide covering prerequisites, .env database configuration, docker-compose startup, local DB schema/trading-calendar initialization, service addresses, hot-reload behavior, and common container operations.

nodes.name: INTEGRATION_GUIDE.md
nodes.summary: REST API integration guide for the Vue frontend team: base URL/CORS setup, full endpoint/response catalogue (health, login, stock info, OHLC, broker summary, insider transactions, crawl logs), error format, and the database-to-backend-to-frontend data pipeline.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Main project README describing StockVision/SahamAI as a Flask + PostgreSQL backend for IDX broker-activity scraping and analysis, with tech stack, project structure, and local setup instructions for both backend and frontend.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Excludes local virtualenvs, caches, and env files from the backend Docker build context.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage python:3.10-slim image that installs backend/requirements.txt, copies the Flask application, exposes port 8080, and runs `python app.py` as the container command.

nodes.name: INTEGRATION_GUIDE.md
nodes.summary: Backend-local, more up-to-date REST API integration guide covering manual/auto-scheduler crawl triggers, OHLC and forecasting endpoints, broker/stock-info/insider query endpoints, crawl-log monitoring, new user/favorites endpoints, and frontend routing/data-pipeline guidance.

nodes.name: app.py
nodes.summary: Main Flask application for StockVision: handles Stockbit authentication/token caching, scrapes and persists broker activity, insider transactions, stock snapshots, OHLC, and orderbook data, exposes the corresponding REST routes, and registers the data_routes and user blueprints plus scheduler control endpoints.
nodes.languageNotes: Registers Flask blueprints (data_bp, user_bp) at import time and imports scheduler control functions directly, while scheduler.py lazily imports back from app.py inside a function body to avoid a circular import.

nodes.name: data_routes.py
nodes.summary: Flask Blueprint (data_bp) exposing read-only query endpoints — historical OHLC/foreign-flow, broker activity, stock info, and majorholder data — that the frontend consumes directly from PostgreSQL.

nodes.name: idxsaham.trading_calendar
nodes.summary: Table recording which calendar dates are IDX trading days, keyed by trading_date; populated by trading_date.py and read by app.py/scheduler.py to resolve valid trading dates.

nodes.name: idxsaham.broker
nodes.summary: Master data table of broker codes and company names, keyed by broker code.

nodes.name: idxsaham.broker_activity
nodes.summary: Daily buy/sell broker transaction summary rows (value, lot, avg price, frequency) per stock/broker/date, indexed by date, symbol, and broker code.

nodes.name: idxsaham.insider_activity
nodes.summary: Insider/majorholder ownership-change transaction records (before/after holdings, source, nationality, brokers involved) keyed by transaction id.

nodes.name: idxsaham.stock_info
nodes.summary: Wide daily snapshot table per symbol capturing price, orderbook depth, trading-limit/margin/haircut flags, corporate action info, and market status, keyed by symbol + date.

nodes.name: trading_date.py
nodes.summary: Standalone CLI script that generates and upserts the IDX trading calendar into the database for one or more years, marking weekends non-trading and pulling national holidays from a public API.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Pinned Python dependencies for the Flask backend: flask, flask-cors, requests, psycopg2-binary, and python-dotenv.

nodes.name: scheduler.py
nodes.summary: Lightweight in-process auto-crawl scheduler built on threading.Timer that checks the IDX trading calendar and market hours, then triggers stock-info and majorholder crawls every 30 minutes, with start/stop/pause/resume/trigger controls exposed to app.py.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a self-rescheduling threading.Timer rather than a cron library, and performs a lazy `from app import ...` inside `_run_scheduled_crawl` to avoid a circular import with app.py, which imports scheduler at module load time.

nodes.name: test_ui.html
nodes.summary: Large standalone HTML/JS test harness page for manually exercising the backend's REST endpoints during development; rendered by user.py's /test-ui route via Flask's render_template.

nodes.name: test_user.py
nodes.summary: unittest suite for user.py covering user creation/retrieval against a mocked psycopg2 connection and full watchlist CRUD lifecycle against a temporary SQLite database.

nodes.name: user.py
nodes.summary: Flask Blueprint (user_bp) handling user account CRUD and authentication against a self-managed PostgreSQL `users` table, plus per-user watchlist CRUD backed by a local SQLite database, and the /test-ui template route.
nodes.languageNotes: Mixes two database backends in one module — PostgreSQL (with its own idempotent DDL) for users, and a local SQLite file for watchlists.

nodes.name: helo.py
nodes.summary: One-line throwaway script that prints 'Hello World'; a leftover scratch/test file with no functional role in the application.

nodes.name: url
nodes.summary: Plain-text scratch list of example StockVision API URLs (auth/login, stock-info, majorholder, broker-activity) kept for manual reference/testing.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose orchestration wiring together the PostgreSQL database, Flask backend, and Vue/Vite frontend containers for local development, with health-check-gated startup ordering.

nodes.name: db
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL 14 (alpine) database service with a healthcheck and a named volume (postgres_data) for persistent storage.

nodes.name: backend
nodes.summary: Flask backend container built from backend/Dockerfile, started only after the db service is healthy, loaded with .env, exposing host port 8080.

nodes.name: frontend
nodes.summary: Vue/Vite dev-server container built from frontend/Dockerfile, depends on the backend service, bind-mounted for hot reload, and exposed on host port 5174.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Excludes node_modules, build output, and local env files from the frontend Docker build context.

nodes.name: .gitignore
nodes.summary: Standard Vue/Vite git-ignore rules excluding node_modules, dist build output, and local env/log files.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: node:20-alpine image that installs npm dependencies, copies the Vue app, exposes port 5173, and runs the Vite dev server bound to all interfaces for containerized local development.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: SahamScope frontend README describing how to run the Vue/Vite dev server, current/planned features, project structure, and notes on connecting to the backend API later.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite HTML entry point for the SahamScope frontend; mounts the Vue app at #app and loads /src/main.js as a module script.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: npm manifest for the SahamScope frontend defining Vite dev/build/preview scripts and dependencies (Vue 3, vue-router, pinia, apexcharts/vue3-apexcharts).

nodes.name: App.vue
nodes.summary: Root Vue component that renders vue-router's <RouterView/>, delegating all page content to the router configuration.

nodes.name: get_last_trading_day
nodes.summary: Walks backward from a target (or current) date through the trading_calendar table to find the most recent valid IDX trading day.

nodes.name: log_crawl_job
nodes.summary: Inserts a crawl job execution record (job type, target, status, record count, error) into the crawl log table for observability.

nodes.name: insert_data_brokers
nodes.summary: Bulk-upserts broker buy/sell activity rows into idxsaham.broker_activity using psycopg2's execute_batch.

nodes.name: insert_data_insider
nodes.summary: Bulk-upserts insider/majorholder transaction rows into idxsaham.insider_activity.

nodes.name: insert_data_stock_info
nodes.summary: Upserts a parsed daily stock snapshot (price, orderbook, corporate action and trading flags) into idxsaham.stock_info.

nodes.name: normalize_number
nodes.summary: Coerces Stockbit's mixed string/number fields into clean int or float values, stripping formatting characters.

nodes.name: _do_login
nodes.summary: Performs the Stockbit username/password login flow and caches the resulting access token with its computed expiry.

nodes.name: _do_refresh
nodes.summary: Refreshes an expired Stockbit access token using a stored refresh token.

nodes.name: get_token
nodes.summary: Returns a valid cached Stockbit access token, transparently refreshing or re-logging in as needed. Reused by scheduler.py's scheduled crawl.

nodes.name: fetch_majorholder
nodes.summary: Paginates the Stockbit majorholder API to collect insider/major-shareholder transaction records for a date range.

nodes.name: fetch_broker_activity
nodes.summary: Fetches paginated buy/sell broker transaction summaries from Stockbit for a symbol/broker/date range, splitting results into buy and sell record lists.

nodes.name: fetch_stock_info
nodes.summary: Calls the Stockbit stock-info endpoint for a given symbol and returns the raw snapshot payload. Reused by scheduler.py's scheduled crawl.

nodes.name: parse_stock_info
nodes.summary: Normalizes a raw Stockbit stock-info payload into the flat field structure stored in idxsaham.stock_info. Reused by scheduler.py's scheduled crawl.

nodes.name: health
nodes.summary: Health-check route reporting Stockbit token cache status and remaining token validity.

nodes.name: force_login
nodes.summary: Route that manually forces a fresh Stockbit login and returns the new token's expiry.

nodes.name: majorholder
nodes.summary: Route handler that resolves the requested date range/token, fetches majorholder data from Stockbit, persists it, and returns a summary JSON response.

nodes.name: broker_activity
nodes.summary: Route handler orchestrating token retrieval, broker-activity fetch, database insert, and JSON response for a broker/date range.

nodes.name: stock_info
nodes.summary: Route handler that fetches, parses, stores, and returns a stock's live snapshot info.

nodes.name: insert_data_ohlc
nodes.summary: Upserts OHLC candle plus foreign-flow rows for a symbol into the database.

nodes.name: get_ohlc
nodes.summary: Route handler returning historical OHLC/foreign-flow chart data for a symbol, crawling fresh data from Stockbit on cache miss and persisting the results.

nodes.name: scheduler_status
nodes.summary: Thin route wrapper that returns scheduler.py's current auto-crawl scheduler status.

nodes.name: scheduler_start
nodes.summary: Route wrapper that starts the background auto-crawl scheduler defined in scheduler.py.

nodes.name: scheduler_stop
nodes.summary: Route wrapper that stops the background auto-crawl scheduler.

nodes.name: scheduler_pause
nodes.summary: Route wrapper that pauses the background auto-crawl scheduler.

nodes.name: scheduler_resume
nodes.summary: Route wrapper that resumes a paused auto-crawl scheduler.

nodes.name: scheduler_trigger
nodes.summary: Route wrapper that forces an immediate out-of-cycle scheduler crawl run.

nodes.name: crawl_status
nodes.summary: Route returning recent crawl job log entries, converting stored timestamps to WIB local time for display.

nodes.name: fetch_orderbook
nodes.summary: Fetches the live bid/offer orderbook depth for a symbol from Stockbit.

nodes.name: insert_data_orderbook
nodes.summary: Bulk-inserts bid/offer orderbook depth rows for a symbol at a given timestamp.

nodes.name: orderbook_batch
nodes.summary: Route handler that crawls orderbook depth for multiple symbols in sequence and persists all results in one batch.

nodes.name: get_historical_ohlc
nodes.summary: Query endpoint returning historical OHLC plus foreign-flow rows for a supported symbol/date range from idxsaham.stock_ohlc for frontend charting.

nodes.name: get_broker_activity
nodes.summary: Query endpoint returning filtered broker buy/sell activity rows from idxsaham.broker_activity for the frontend broker-summary view.

nodes.name: get_stock_info
nodes.summary: Query endpoint returning the latest stored stock snapshot for a symbol from idxsaham.stock_info.

nodes.name: get_majorholder_data
nodes.summary: Query endpoint returning paginated insider/majorholder transaction rows for a symbol from idxsaham.insider_activity.

nodes.name: fetch_holidays_from_api
nodes.summary: Fetches Indonesian national public holidays for a given year from the libur.deno.dev public API.

nodes.name: generate_calendar
nodes.summary: Generates and upserts a full year's IDX trading-day calendar (marking weekends and fetched holidays as non-trading) into idxsaham.trading_calendar.

nodes.name: is_trading_day
nodes.summary: Checks idxsaham.trading_calendar (falling back to a weekday heuristic) to determine whether a date is an IDX trading day.

nodes.name: get_next_trading_day
nodes.summary: Looks up the next scheduled trading day after a given date from idxsaham.trading_calendar.

nodes.name: _log_crawl
nodes.summary: Writes a scheduler-triggered crawl job's outcome (status, target, error) into the crawl log table.

nodes.name: _run_scheduled_crawl
nodes.summary: Core scheduled job: verifies it's a trading day and within market hours, then imports and calls app.py's crawl functions to refresh stock-info and majorholder data, logging outcomes and errors.
nodes.languageNotes: Imports get_token, fetch_stock_info, parse_stock_info, insert_data_stock_info, fetch_majorholder, and insert_data_insider from app.py lazily inside this function body to sidestep the circular import between app.py and scheduler.py.

nodes.name: _schedule_next
nodes.summary: Schedules the next recursive threading.Timer invocation of the crawl job loop.

nodes.name: start_scheduler
nodes.summary: Starts the background auto-crawl loop if it is not already running. Called from app.py's /scheduler/start route.

nodes.name: stop_scheduler
nodes.summary: Cancels the pending timer and stops the auto-crawl loop. Called from app.py's /scheduler/stop route.

nodes.name: pause_scheduler
nodes.summary: Pauses the auto-crawl loop without fully stopping it. Called from app.py's /scheduler/pause route.

nodes.name: resume_scheduler
nodes.summary: Resumes a paused auto-crawl loop. Called from app.py's /scheduler/resume route.

nodes.name: trigger_now
nodes.summary: Forces an immediate out-of-cycle crawl run. Called from app.py's /scheduler/trigger route.

nodes.name: get_scheduler_status
nodes.summary: Reports the scheduler's running/paused state, next scheduled run, and recent crawl history. Called from app.py's /scheduler/status route.

nodes.name: _ensure_users_table
nodes.summary: Idempotently creates the PostgreSQL `users` table and its indexes/constraints on first use.

nodes.name: create_user
nodes.summary: Validates the request payload and inserts a new user record with a hashed password.

nodes.name: get_user
nodes.summary: Fetches a single user record by id.

nodes.name: get_users
nodes.summary: Lists all users in the system.

nodes.name: _ensure_watchlist_db
nodes.summary: Idempotently creates the local SQLite watchlist database schema on first use.

nodes.name: create_watchlist
nodes.summary: Creates a new watchlist (with its stock symbols) for a user in the local SQLite database.

nodes.name: get_watchlists
nodes.summary: Lists all watchlists (with their symbols) belonging to a user.

nodes.name: get_watchlist
nodes.summary: Fetches a single watchlist by id, scoped to its owning user.

nodes.name: update_watchlist
nodes.summary: Updates a watchlist's name and/or symbol list.

nodes.name: delete_watchlist
nodes.summary: Deletes a watchlist and its associated symbols.

nodes.name: update_user
nodes.summary: Updates a user's profile fields, re-hashing the password if it was changed.

nodes.name: login_user
nodes.summary: Validates submitted credentials against the stored password hash and returns the user record on success.

nodes.name: delete_user
nodes.summary: Deletes a user record by id.

nodes.name: FakeCursor
nodes.summary: Minimal in-memory stand-in for a psycopg2 cursor, used to unit-test user.py's SQL calls without a real database connection.

nodes.name: FakeConnection
nodes.summary: Fake database connection pairing with FakeCursor to intercept user.py's get_connection() calls during tests.

nodes.name: UserCrudTests
nodes.summary: unittest.TestCase covering user creation/retrieval (against a FakeConnection) and the full watchlist CRUD lifecycle (against a real temporary SQLite file).

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet defining the app's dark navy/teal design-system CSS custom properties (colors, radii, shadows) and shared layout utilities (.card, .pill, .dtable, .btn) reused across every Vue view.

nodes.name: AreaChart.vue
nodes.summary: Reusable ApexCharts area/line chart wrapper component that derives chart options (colors, fill, axes, tooltip) from a `series` prop using the shared chart theme.

nodes.name: BarChart.vue
nodes.summary: Reusable ApexCharts bar chart wrapper supporting horizontal orientation and per-bar distributed coloring, used for broker net-lot and volume visualizations.

nodes.name: CandleChart.vue
nodes.summary: Reusable ApexCharts candlestick chart wrapper rendering OHLC price bars with optional moving-average overlay lines.

nodes.name: chartTheme.js
nodes.summary: Shared ApexCharts styling constants (brand colors, base chart config, axis/grid style) applied consistently across all chart wrapper components.

nodes.name: axisStyle
nodes.summary: Returns shared ApexCharts axis label styling reused by all chart wrapper components.

nodes.name: StatCard.vue
nodes.summary: Reusable stat-tile UI component displaying a labeled metric value with an optional icon, percentage-change pill, and sublabel; not currently imported by any view in this batch.

nodes.name: market.js
nodes.summary: Deterministic mock market-data generator providing seeded-random OHLC candles, quotes, order books, broker summaries, insider activity, technical indicators (SMA/RSI), and forecast series for the IDX demo dataset.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a mulberry32 PRNG seeded from the ticker string so mock data is stable per-ticker across re-renders.

nodes.name: getEmiten
nodes.summary: Looks up a single emiten (listed company) record by ticker code from the static universe list.

nodes.name: generateOHLC
nodes.summary: Generates a seeded-random sequence of daily OHLCV candles for a ticker over a given number of trading days, skipping weekends.

nodes.name: latestQuote
nodes.summary: Derives the latest quote (price, change, changePct) for a ticker from its most recently generated OHLC candle.

nodes.name: marketOverview
nodes.summary: Builds market-wide summary stats, including top gainers/losers and sector performance, aggregated across all quotes.

nodes.name: tickSize
nodes.summary: Returns the IDX tick size (minimum price increment) applicable to a given price level.

nodes.name: terminalStats
nodes.summary: Computes trading-terminal statistics (frequency, value, bid ratio, OHLC) used in the dashboard ticker header.

nodes.name: orderBook
nodes.summary: Generates a seeded mock bid/offer order book with cumulative lot totals for a ticker.

nodes.name: doneTrades
nodes.summary: Generates a seeded list of recent executed trade ticks (done trades) for a ticker.

nodes.name: brokerSummary
nodes.summary: Generates mock per-broker net buy/sell activity rows and derives the top buyer/seller lists for a ticker.

nodes.name: insiderActivity
nodes.summary: Generates a seeded list of mock insider (director/commissioner) buy/sell transactions for a ticker.

nodes.name: sma
nodes.summary: Computes a simple moving average series over OHLC closes for a given period window.

nodes.name: rsi
nodes.summary: Computes the Relative Strength Index (RSI) technical indicator over OHLC closes.

nodes.name: forecast
nodes.summary: Produces a linear-regression-based price forecast with confidence bands, projected value, and trend slope for a ticker.

nodes.name: seedCrawlJobs
nodes.summary: Generates the initial set of mock crawler job records (per emiten/source) with randomized status and progress for the Crawling Monitor view.

nodes.name: AppLayout.vue
nodes.summary: Root authenticated layout shell that combines the topbar navigation with routed page content via `<RouterView>`.

nodes.name: AppTopbar.vue
nodes.summary: Global navigation topbar showing the brand, a ticker selector bound to the market store, a notification icon, and a user profile dropdown with logout action.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: Application entry point that bootstraps the Vue app with Pinia, Vue Router, and the ApexCharts plugin, then mounts it to the DOM.

nodes.name: index.js
nodes.summary: Vue Router configuration defining the login and authenticated dashboard routes, with a global navigation guard that redirects based on auth state.

nodes.name: authService.js
nodes.summary: Authentication service that POSTs credentials to the backend login endpoint, maps the response into a camelCase user object, and throws typed `AuthError`s for invalid credentials, server errors, or network failures.

nodes.name: AuthError
nodes.summary: Custom error class carrying a machine-readable `code` field so the UI can branch on failure type (invalid credentials, server error, network failure).

nodes.name: login
nodes.summary: Performs a login POST request against the backend `/users/login` endpoint, maps the response to a camelCase user object, and throws typed `AuthError`s on failure.

nodes.name: auth.js
nodes.summary: Pinia store managing authenticated user/token state, persisting it to localStorage, and orchestrating login/logout including lazily-loaded watchlist syncing with the market store.

nodes.name: market.js
nodes.summary: Pinia store providing the selected ticker, market universe/quote lookups, and watchlist state synced with backend `/users/:id/watchlists` API endpoints.

nodes.name: format.js
nodes.summary: Indonesian-locale formatting helpers for currency (rupiah), compact rupiah notation, numbers, percentages, and relative time strings, used throughout the dashboard views.

nodes.name: rupiah
nodes.summary: Formats a number as Indonesian Rupiah currency using `Intl.NumberFormat`.

nodes.name: rupiahShort
nodes.summary: Formats a large number as a compact Rupiah string using T/M/jt/rb magnitude suffixes.

nodes.name: number
nodes.summary: Formats a number using Indonesian locale digit grouping.

nodes.name: pct
nodes.summary: Formats a number as a signed percentage string.

nodes.name: timeAgo
nodes.summary: Converts an ISO timestamp into a relative 'time ago' string in Indonesian.

nodes.name: BrokerSummaryView.vue
nodes.summary: Broker Summary tab view showing net-buy/net-sell totals, a per-broker net-lot bar chart, and top-buyer/top-seller tables for the selected ticker.

nodes.name: CrawlingView.vue
nodes.summary: Crawling Monitor view simulating live crawler job status/progress with retry and run-all controls, polling every 1.5 seconds via `setInterval`.

nodes.name: DashboardView.vue
nodes.summary: Main trading-terminal dashboard combining the ticker header, candlestick chart, order buttons, orderbook/done-trades panels, and a tabbed nav bar that swaps in the other view components (broker, price, technical, forecasting, insider, crawling, watchlist).

nodes.name: ForecastingView.vue
nodes.summary: Forecasting tab view rendering a projected price band (historical + forecast + confidence bounds) with a selectable horizon (10/20/40 days).

nodes.name: InsiderView.vue
nodes.summary: Insider & Backdoor Activity view listing insider transactions with buy/sell filters, net-flow totals, and a distribution-alert banner.

nodes.name: LoginView.vue
nodes.summary: Login page with a branded live-chart preview panel and a validated email/password form supporting demo-account autofill and typed error handling via `AuthError`.

nodes.name: PricesView.vue
nodes.summary: Daily Price view showing an OHLCV candlestick chart with optional MA20/MA50 overlays, a selectable time range, and a volume bar chart.

nodes.name: TechnicalView.vue
nodes.summary: Technical Analysis view presenting four analytic layers (descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, prescriptive) with RSI/MA computations, forecast bands, and a rule-based buy/hold/sell recommendation.

nodes.name: WatchlistView.vue
nodes.summary: Watchlist Crawler management view providing CRUD (add/edit/remove/toggle-enabled) for emiten crawl configuration via a modal form bound to the market store.

nodes.name: vite.config.js
nodes.summary: Vite build configuration for the frontend, registering the Vue plugin, defining the `@` path alias to `src`, and setting the dev server port/auto-open behavior.

layers.name: Backend API Layer
layers.description: Flask entry point and REST endpoints for broker activity, insider/major-holder transactions, stock info, OHLC/orderbook data, and user account and watchlist management, plus the pinned Python dependency manifest that configures the Flask app.

layers.name: Backend Data Layer
layers.description: PostgreSQL schema definitions for the idxsaham database, covering the trading calendar, broker master data, broker activity, insider/major-holder activity, and daily stock-info snapshots.

layers.name: Data Pipeline & Scraping Scripts
layers.description: Standalone background jobs that populate IDX data: the trading-calendar seeding script and the in-process auto-crawl scheduler that periodically triggers Stockbit scraping through the API layer.

layers.name: Backend Testing & Dev Tools
layers.description: Unit tests for the user module, a manual HTML/JS test harness for exercising REST endpoints, and leftover scratch scripts/notes kept for backend development reference.

layers.name: Frontend UI Layer
layers.description: Vue 3 presentation layer: the dashboard's nine page-views, the app shell/layout/topbar, reusable ApexCharts chart components with their shared theme, the stat-card widget, and the global stylesheet.

layers.name: Frontend State & Service Layer
layers.description: Pinia stores for auth and market state, the backend-auth HTTP service, and the deterministic mock market-data engine that currently powers the dashboard's charts and tables.

layers.name: Frontend Bootstrap & Build Config
layers.description: Vue app bootstrap and router configuration, the Vite HTML entry point and build config, and the npm package manifest/git-ignore rules for the SahamScope frontend.

layers.name: Frontend Utility Layer
layers.description: Shared Indonesian-locale formatting helpers for currency, numbers, percentages, and relative time, reused throughout the dashboard views.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project-level and API integration documentation: the root and frontend READMEs plus the root and backend-local REST API integration guides.

layers.name: Infrastructure & Deployment
layers.description: Docker packaging for the backend and frontend, the docker-compose orchestration wiring together the PostgreSQL database, Flask backend, and Vue frontend containers, and the Docker build-context ignore rules.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start here to understand what StockVision-SahamAI is building: a Flask + PostgreSQL backend that scrapes and serves Indonesian Stock Exchange (IDX) broker-activity data, paired with a Vue 3 frontend called SahamScope. The README documents both stacks' tech choices (Python 3.10+, PostgreSQL 14+, Selenium for scraping) and local setup steps, giving you the map before diving into the code. Note that its project-structure diagram is stale — the codebase has since been reorganized into backend/ and frontend/ directories with Docker packaging added, which this tour reflects.

tour.title: Backend Entry Point: Flask API & Scraper Core
tour.description: backend/app.py is the heart of the backend: it authenticates against Stockbit, scrapes broker activity, insider transactions, stock snapshots, OHLC, and orderbook data, exposes the corresponding REST routes, and registers the data_routes and user blueprints plus the scheduler's control endpoints. Its dependencies are pinned in requirements.txt (Flask, flask-cors, requests, psycopg2-binary, python-dotenv) — a small, focused stack for a scraping-and-serving API. The topology analysis flagged this file as the top code entry point: it has the highest fan-out on the backend side and is where request handling, authentication, and data ingestion all converge.
tour.languageLesson: app.py registers Flask Blueprints (data_bp, user_bp) at import time, while scheduler.py imports functions back from app.py lazily inside a function body — a common Python pattern for breaking circular imports between two tightly coupled modules.

tour.title: Backend REST Blueprints: Queries & User Accounts
tour.description: Following app.py's imports (the first ring of the dependency graph), two Blueprint modules split responsibilities: data_routes.py exposes read-only query endpoints (historical OHLC/foreign-flow, broker activity, stock info, majorholder data) that the frontend consumes directly from PostgreSQL, while user.py handles user account CRUD/authentication plus per-user watchlist management. Notably, user.py mixes two database backends in one module — PostgreSQL for the shared users table and a local SQLite file for watchlists — a pragmatic split introduced as the feature grew. Both were registered as blueprints inside Step 2's app.py.
tour.languageLesson: Flask Blueprints let you split a monolithic app.py into focused, independently-routed modules that then get wired together with app.register_blueprint() in the main file.

tour.title: Data Pipeline: Trading Calendar & Auto-Crawl Scheduler
tour.description: Two standalone background jobs keep the database fresh. trading_date.py is a CLI script that seeds the IDX trading calendar for one or more years, marking weekends non-trading and pulling national holidays from a public API. scheduler.py is an in-process auto-crawl loop (built on threading.Timer, not a cron library) that checks this calendar and market hours, then triggers stock-info and majorholder crawls every 30 minutes by calling back into app.py's fetch/insert functions from Step 2.
tour.languageLesson: scheduler.py performs a lazy `from app import ...` inside its `_run_scheduled_crawl` function rather than at module load time, sidestepping the circular import that would otherwise occur since app.py imports scheduler.py when the app starts.

tour.title: Database Schema: The idxsaham Tables
tour.description: These PostgreSQL tables are what all the ingestion code from Steps 2-4 ultimately writes to, and what data_routes.py reads from. trading_calendar backs the trading-day lookups used by app.py and scheduler.py; broker_activity and insider_activity store the daily buy/sell and ownership-change transactions scraped from Stockbit; stock_info is a wide daily snapshot per symbol with price, orderbook depth, and corporate-action flags. Together they form the persistent record that turns raw scraped API responses into a queryable trading-activity database.
tour.languageLesson: This schema enforces data integrity through composite keys (symbol + date) and indexes on the columns queries filter on most — date, symbol, and broker code — keeping broker-activity lookups fast as the table grows.

tour.title: Backend Testing & Dev Tools
tour.description: test_user.py is the project's real unit-test suite, covering user.py's user creation/retrieval against a mocked psycopg2 connection and the full watchlist CRUD lifecycle against a temporary SQLite database — validating the dual-database design introduced in Step 3. test_ui.html is a different kind of tool: a large standalone HTML/JS harness page rendered via a Flask route, used for manually exercising REST endpoints during development. Together they show the project's pragmatic, lightweight approach to backend quality assurance.
tour.languageLesson: Python's unittest module combined with lightweight FakeCursor/FakeConnection stand-ins lets you test SQL-calling code without a real database connection — a common technique for isolating database logic in tests.

tour.title: API Contract: Integration Guides
tour.description: Before crossing into the frontend, these documents capture the REST API contract that Steps 2-4's endpoints expose. The root INTEGRATION_GUIDE.md documents base URL/CORS setup and the endpoint/response catalogue for the Vue team, while the backend-local copy is the more current version, additionally covering the scheduler's manual/auto crawl triggers, forecasting endpoints, and newer user/favorites endpoints. Reading these clarifies exactly what shape of JSON the frontend, starting next, expects to receive from the backend you just toured.

tour.title: Frontend Bootstrap: Vite Entry & Build Config
tour.description: Just as backend/app.py was the backend's entry point, frontend/index.html is where the Vue app starts: a minimal HTML shell that mounts the app at #app and loads /src/main.js as an ES module. main.js bootstraps Vue with Pinia (state), Vue Router (navigation), and the ApexCharts plugin before mounting to the DOM. vite.config.js configures the dev server and defines the `@` path alias to src/, which you'll see used throughout the frontend imports ahead.
tour.languageLesson: Vite entry points use a native ES module `<script type="module">` tag instead of a bundler-injected script — the browser resolves imports directly during development, which is what enables Vite's fast hot-module-reload.

tour.title: Routing, Auth Store & App Shell
tour.description: router/index.js defines the login and authenticated dashboard routes with a global navigation guard that redirects based on auth state — the frontend's top entry-point candidate after main.js. App.vue is intentionally thin, just rendering <RouterView/> and delegating all page content to this router. The auth.js Pinia store manages the logged-in user/token, persists it to localStorage, and calls authService.js to POST credentials to the backend's /users/login endpoint from Step 3, throwing a typed AuthError on failure.
tour.languageLesson: Pinia stores are plain functions wrapped in defineStore() that return reactive state, getters, and actions — no boilerplate mutations/reducers like older state libraries.

tour.title: Market State & Mock Data Engine
tour.description: stores/market.js and data/market.js are the two most depended-upon files in the entire frontend (fan-in of 10 and 9 respectively) — nearly every view you'll see next imports one or both. The Pinia store exposes the selected ticker, market universe/quote lookups, and watchlist state synced with the backend's /users/:id/watchlists endpoints, while data/market.js is a deterministic mock-data generator that currently powers the dashboard's charts and tables with seeded-random OHLC candles, order books, broker summaries, and technical indicators. This mock layer is a placeholder for the real backend data described in Step 7's integration guide.
tour.languageLesson: data/market.js uses a mulberry32 PRNG seeded from each ticker string, so the 'random' mock data is actually stable and reproducible per-ticker across re-renders — a useful trick for demo data that needs to look consistent without a real backend.

tour.title: Dashboard Shell & Orchestrator View
tour.description: AppLayout.vue combines the topbar navigation with routed page content, and AppTopbar.vue reads from the auth and market stores from Steps 9-10 to render the ticker selector and user profile dropdown. DashboardView.vue is the frontend's highest fan-out node (11 outgoing dependencies) — the trading-terminal orchestrator that combines the ticker header, candlestick chart, orderbook/done-trades panels, and a tabbed nav bar that swaps in each of the other view components (broker summary, prices, technical, forecasting, insider, crawling, watchlist).
tour.languageLesson: DashboardView.vue acts as a compound-component orchestrator: instead of each feature being a separate route, they're tab-switched children rendered conditionally inside one parent, sharing the same layout and avoiding router re-mounts.

tour.title: Reusable Charts & Shared Utilities
tour.description: chartTheme.js centralizes ApexCharts styling constants (colors, axis/grid style) so that CandleChart.vue and AreaChart.vue render with a consistent look — each is a thin wrapper that derives chart options from a `series` prop. format.js is the frontend's third most depended-upon file (fan-in of 8), providing Indonesian-locale helpers for currency (rupiah), compact numbers, percentages, and relative time, used across nearly every view wired into DashboardView in Step 11.
tour.languageLesson: Wrapper components like CandleChart.vue take a generic `series` prop and derive fully-configured chart options internally — this keeps chart-library specifics out of the view components, which only ever pass data.

tour.title: Containerization: Dockerfiles & Compose Orchestration
tour.description: With both stacks toured, here's how they get packaged. backend/Dockerfile builds a python:3.10-slim image that installs requirements.txt (Step 2) and runs `python app.py` on port 8080. frontend/Dockerfile builds a node:20-alpine image running the Vite dev server on port 5173. docker-compose.yml wires both together with a PostgreSQL 14 database service, using health-check-gated startup ordering so the backend waits for the database and the frontend waits for the backend.
tour.languageLesson: docker-compose's `depends_on: condition: service_healthy`, paired with a healthcheck on the db service, ensures the backend doesn't start querying PostgreSQL before it's actually ready to accept connections — a common race condition in naive multi-container setups.

tour.title: Deployment Guide: DOCKER.md
tour.description: This guide closes the loop on Step 13's compose setup: it walks through prerequisites, .env database configuration, docker-compose startup, local DB schema and trading-calendar initialization (Step 4's trading_date.py), service addresses, hot-reload behavior, and common container operations. It's the practical, day-to-day reference for anyone running this project locally after understanding the architecture covered in this tour.
